pool.baseline:
hey i just got this nda from a potential partner and i dont really want to pay a lawyer just to read it. can u look it over and tell me the main things i actually need to worry about? like how long it lasts and what exactly i cant talk about? 

here is the text:

[TEXT START]
NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT
This Non-Disclosure Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of October 12, 2023, by and between Zenith Solutions LLC ("Disclosing Party") and Miller Creative Agency ("Receiving Party").

1. Definition of Confidential Information. "Confidential Information" shall include all non-public, proprietary, or sensitive information disclosed by Disclosing Party, whether oral or written, including but not limited to business plans, client lists, software code, and financial data.

2. Obligations of Receiving Party. The Receiving Party shall hold the Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall not disclose such information to any third party without prior written consent. Receiving Party shall use the same degree of care to protect the Confidential Information as it uses to protect its own confidential information of a similar nature, but in no event less than reasonable care.

3. Term and Termination. This Agreement shall remain in effect for a period of three (3) years from the date of disclosure, notwithstanding the termination of any business relationship between the parties.

4. Remedies. The Receiving Party acknowledges that any unauthorized disclosure may cause irreparable harm to the Disclosing Party, for which money damages may be inadequate, and the Disclosing Party shall be entitled to seek injunctive relief.
[TEXT END]
